Home / Alternatives to Mixpanel / Mixpanel vs Plausible

Mixpanel vs Plausible

A side-by-side look at Mixpanel (the paid SaaS) and Plausible (the open source alternative). Use this page to decide if the switch fits your team and workflow.

Mixpanel Plausible
Tagline Product analytics and funnels. Lightweight, privacy-first Google Analytics alternative.
License Proprietary SaaS AGPL-3.0
Pricing Free up to 20M events/month; Growth from $28/month. Free to self-host · optional paid hosted plan
Self-host option No Yes — difficulty 2/5
Hosted cloud available Yes (only option) Yes
Desktop apps Varies by product Web only
Mobile apps Official apps typically available None official
Ad slot — between tables

Best for

Simple web-level metrics without the cost.

Plausible strengths

  • No cookies — no consent banner needed.
  • Tiny script, fast page loads.
  • Clean, simple dashboard.

Plausible weaknesses

  • Lighter feature set than GA4 (no funnels in free tier).
  • Retention limits on cloud tier.
  • Some goal-tracking requires plan upgrade.

What's the catch with Mixpanel?

  • Pricing grows steeply beyond free tier.
  • No self-hosted option.
  • Overkill for simple web traffic.

Still unsure?

Check the full list of alternatives to Mixpanel: see Mixpanel alternatives, or learn more about Plausible on its project page.

OpenInstead — open source alternatives to the SaaS you already use. Updated April 2026.
Disclaimer: licensing, features and pricing change. Verify each tool's current status on its official site before committing.
